trade.summary: Bilateral trade has effectively collapsed. Poland was among the first EU countries to advocate for the harshest possible sanctions against Russia following the 2022 Ukraine invasion. Poland severed virtually all commercial ties. Pre-2022, Russia was a significant supplier of energy (gas, oil, coal) to Poland — all eliminated. Poland closed its airspace to Russian aircraft. Residual trade involves only permitted humanitarian or essential categories.
disputes: Russia sanctioned Polish officials and companies
Disputes over Smolensk crash investigation access
Poland closed border crossings to Russian and Belarusian trucks

diplomatic.status: near-complete breakdown — embassies operational at minimal staff
diplomatic.summary: Poland-Russia relations are among the most hostile bilateral relationships in Europe. Deep historical grievances (Katyn massacre, Soviet occupation, Smolensk crash) predate the current crisis. Since 2022, Poland has expelled Russian diplomats, designated Russia as a terrorist state, confiscated Russian assets, and is the leading voice in NATO and the EU for maximum pressure on Moscow. Poland hosts the largest Ukrainian refugee population in Europe (~1.5 million) and has supplied billions in military aid to Ukraine. Russia has responded with sanctions, disinformation campaigns, and threats against Polish territory.
recent_incidents: Russia placed Polish officials on most-wanted lists
Smolensk Tu-154 crash investigation — Poland accused Russia of obstruction and destruction of evidence
Russian disinformation campaigns targeting Polish politics
Russian threats to redeploy tactical nuclear weapons near Polish border

military.summary: Poland is NATO's most important eastern flank state, hosting the largest US military presence in Europe (~10,000+ US troops). Poland has the largest army in continental NATO Europe by headcount and is on a massive rearmament trajectory (targeting 4%+ GDP on defense). Russia considers Poland a primary adversary. Incidents of Russian military probing (electronic warfare, GPS jamming, airspace violations) occur regularly near Polish-Belarusian border. Poland participated in tracking Russian missile and drone trajectories during Ukrainian attacks.
military.alliance_status: adversarial — Poland is NATO, Russia is designated adversary
incidents: Russian missiles and drones violated Polish airspace during attacks on Ukraine
Russian GPS jamming affecting Polish aviation
Russian electronic warfare operations near Polish borders
Russia moved tactical nuclear weapons to Belarus near Polish border

financial.summary: Financial relations are essentially severed. All banking transactions subject to sanctions. Polish banks have no Russian exposure. Poland has advocated for confiscating frozen Russian central bank assets to fund Ukrainian reconstruction. Polish companies have exited Russian markets. Russia has confiscated assets of Polish companies operating in Russia.

energy.summary: Poland has completely eliminated Russian energy dependency — a goal pursued since 2022. Russian pipeline gas (Yamal-Europe pipeline) deliveries to Poland were terminated; Poland sourced LNG from US, Qatar, and Norwegian gas via Baltic Pipe. Russian oil imports eliminated through EU embargo. Russian coal banned. Poland's energy decarbonization from Russia is now complete and celebrated as a strategic achievement.

scientific.summary: All scientific cooperation has been suspended. Polish universities have terminated partnerships with Russian institutions. Polish scientists have left international collaborations involving Russian institutions. Poland advocates for excluding Russian scientists from global conferences and publications related to dual-use research.
